title: max_steps: 3 runs EXACTLY 3 turns and stops with reason='turn_limit'
description:
SPEC.md §9.2: 'max_steps: N runs EXACTLY N turns.' This is the strictest boundary promise — unlike cost/tokens which MAY overshoot by one final turn, the step projection is checked BEFORE starting the next would-be turn (state.total_turns + 1) > max_steps so the run terminates ahead of overshoot.

This case scripts 5 model responses but sets max_steps=3. The runner MUST execute exactly 3 turns and emit agent_stopped with reason='turn_limit'. SPEC.md §9.4 also requires two conforming runners with identical inputs to agree on this — pinning it normatively here lets translator runners (which delegate turn-counting to their SDK in some configurations) be checked against the canonical answer.

forbidden_events.label: the run MUST stop at exactly 3 turns — no 4th model_turn_started may appear
